bins.name: AI leadership drives reform
bins.desc: OpenAI frames the rulemaking as essential to U.S. competitiveness in AI and supports the DOE and Commission initiative to promote speed, reliability, and clarity in large-load interconnection.
quotes: OpenAI supports the DOE's and the Commission's initiative to promote speed, reliability and clarity in the interconnection of large loads to the interstate transmission system.

bins.name: Fast-track high-value loads
bins.desc: OpenAI wants designated national-interest projects to receive a required fast-track interconnection process, and supports expedited treatment for loads that accept curtailment.
quotes: OpenAI supports an approach that allows the DOE to identify and provide a "national-interest" designation for loads above 250 MW that have clear national interest or security,economicimperativeorcompetitiveness,orcritical-infrastructurevalue
Transmission providers shouldthenberequiredtoexpeditetheinterconnectionofthesedesignatedprojects through a fast track process.
OpenAIsupportsanoptionalcurtailable-loadpathwaywherefullorpartialcurtailment obligations of the large loads allow thetransmissionprovidertoexpeditetheinterconnection request.

bins.name: 250 MW national-interest threshold
bins.desc: OpenAI proposes that DOE designate loads above 250 MW with national-interest, security, economic, or critical-infrastructure value for expedited treatment.
quotes: OpenAI supports an approach that allows the DOE to identify and provide a "national-interest" designation for loads above 250 MW that have clear national interest or security,economicimperativeorcompetitiveness,orcritical-infrastructurevalue

bins.name: Curtailable load priority
bins.desc: OpenAI supports an optional curtailable-load pathway and argues loads that agree to curtail on operator call should get interconnection priority over inflexible loads.
quotes: OpenAIsupportsanoptionalcurtailable-loadpathwaywherefullorpartialcurtailment obligations of the large loads allow thetransmissionprovidertoexpeditetheinterconnection request.
Largeloadsthatagreetocurtaildemandwhencalledonbysystemoperatorsfor a predetermined number of hours should be given interconnection priority over inflexible loads.

bins.name: Net-impact study for hybrids
bins.desc: OpenAI supports a pathway for load paired with on-site or behind-the-meter generation to be evaluated on a net-impact basis to capture real system effects and cut study burden.
quotes: OpenAI supports a clear pathway for load paired with on-site or behind-the-meter generationtobeevaluatedonanet-impactbasistocapturerealsystemeffectsandreducestudy burdensandduration.

bins.name: On-site and behind-meter pairing
bins.desc: OpenAI backs co-located load-plus-generation configurations, asking that on-site or behind-the-meter generation be credited through net-impact evaluation.
quotes: OpenAI supports a clear pathway for load paired with on-site or behind-the-meter generationtobeevaluatedonanet-impactbasistocapturerealsystemeffectsandreducestudy burdensandduration.

bins.name: Readiness-based study and prioritization
bins.desc: OpenAI supports standardized readiness criteria using strict commercial-readiness metrics, and net-impact studies that reduce study duration, to direct scarce study resources to vetted projects.
quotes: OpenAI supports a clear pathway for load paired with on-site or behind-the-meter generationtobeevaluatedonanet-impactbasistocapturerealsystemeffectsandreducestudy burdensandduration.

OpenAI supports standardized readiness criteria that classify large loads based on strict metrics for commercial readiness, end user alignment, site and engineering maturity, and near-term operational timelines.

bins.name: Tiered withdrawal penalties
bins.desc: OpenAI wants reduced withdrawal penalties for ready projects with valid end-user agreements that withdraw for reasons beyond their control, while keeping full penalties for speculative loads without offtake agreements.
quotes: Forlargeloadprojectsthatmeetthereadinesscriteriaandhavesecuredvalidenduser agreements but subsequently withdraw for reasonsbeyondtheircontrol,withdrawalpenalties shouldbereduced.
Speculativeloadsthatwithdrawwithoutenduserofftakeagreementsshould remain subject to full withdrawal penalties.

bins.name: Self-fund network upgrades
bins.desc: OpenAI supports letting commercially-ready loads fund and voluntarily build required network upgrades, subject to appropriate protections, when doing so accelerates interconnection.

bins.name: Load-funded upgrade costs
bins.desc: OpenAI proposes that ready large loads bear the cost of constructing required network upgrades themselves to speed their interconnection.
